grants.title: ON-RAMP Program
grants.summary: Funds the creation of four new workforce development centers in Upstate New York.
grants.description: The $200 million ON-RAMP Program aims to establish a network of four workforce development centers in strategic, high-impact locations across Upstate New York. These centers will focus on preparing workers with skills for high-quality, in-demand jobs, supporting regional economic growth, and addressing the current and future needs of businesses and industries. The program is part of New York State's broader workforce development efforts. Interested applicants can learn more through the provided resources and contact Empire State Development for further details.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations involved in workforce development and regional economic growth may apply.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Focus on workforce development
Located in Upstate New York
Ability to address high-demand job training
Support regional economic growth

grants.title: ConnectALL Initiative
grants.summary: The largest-ever NYS digital infrastructure investment aimed at delivering affordable broadband to New Yorkers.
grants.description: The ConnectALL Initiative is a $1 billion investment by New York State to transform digital infrastructure and provide affordable broadband access to residents. This program supports the state's innovation economy and aims to bridge the digital divide, ensuring equitable access to technology for all New Yorkers. The initiative is part of broader infrastructure efforts, including renewable energy and transportation projects. Interested parties can learn more through the Empire State Development website.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations involved in digital infrastructure and broadband access projects may apply.
key_requirements: Projects must focus on digital infrastructure development.
Applicants must demonstrate capacity to deliver affordable broadband access.
Organizations must align with New York State's economic development goals.

grants.title: New York State Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI)
grants.summary: Provides over $500 million in federal funding to support small and disadvantaged businesses in New York State, focusing on post-pandemic recovery.
grants.description: The New York State Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI) offers over $500 million in federal funding to help small businesses and traditionally disadvantaged businesses succeed. The program focuses on post-pandemic recovery and includes various initiatives such as loans, equity investments, and technical assistance. Eligible businesses can access affordable capital, expert services, and resources to grow and succeed. Applications can be submitted through participating lenders or program-specific portals.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include small businesses and traditionally disadvantaged businesses in New York State.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Business must be located in New York State
Must qualify as a small or disadvantaged business
Focus on post-pandemic recovery initiatives

grants.title: Innovation Development Support
grants.summary: This grant supports businesses in New York State to become more competitive through innovative technologies and collaboration with industry and universities.
grants.description: The Innovation Development Support program, offered by Empire State Development's Division of Science, Technology and Innovation (NYSTAR), provides resources to help businesses leverage New York State’s technology strengths. It includes financial incentives, access to NYSTAR's 70+ funded facilities, and tools for growth. The program fosters university collaboration, research, and innovation to produce new products. Businesses can apply through various NYSTAR programs, including incubators, accelerators, and matching grants.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include businesses operating in New York State that aim to leverage innovative technologies and collaborate with universities or industry.
key_requirements: Business must operate in New York State
Focus on leveraging innovative technologies
Collaboration with universities or industry partners

grants.title: Global NY Export Assistance
grants.summary: This grant provides funding and technical assistance to help New York State businesses expand their presence in international markets.
grants.description: Global NY Export Assistance offers financial and technical support to New York State businesses to help them connect with international markets. Programs include grants and loans to offset exporting costs, trade missions to build global partnerships, and services to find sales agents and distributors abroad. Eligible businesses can apply for funding through the STEP program or the Global NY Grant Fund Program. Applications are open to small businesses and nonprofits looking to begin or increase exports.
grants.award_text: Up to $10,000 through STEP program; up to $25,000 for small businesses and up to $100,000 for nonprofits through Global NY Grant Fund Program.
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to New York State small businesses and nonprofits looking to begin exporting or increase global exports.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Applicant must be a New York State-based small business or nonprofit.
Applicant must be seeking to begin exporting or increase global exports.
Funding is available for eligible export-related activities.

grants.title: International Investment | Empire State Development
grants.summary: This grant supports international businesses looking to establish or expand operations in New York State.
grants.description: Empire State Development's foreign direct investment (FDI) team provides resources and support to international businesses at any stage of development to help them grow and prosper in New York State. The program highlights New York's strategic location, world-class infrastructure, skilled workforce, and quality of life as key advantages for global companies. Businesses can explore opportunities such as competitions, incentives, and infrastructure investments to establish or expand their presence in the state. Interested applicants can contact the FDI team for guidance and further information.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include international businesses seeking to establish or expand operations in New York State.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: International business seeking to operate in New York State
Commitment to growth and innovation
Ability to leverage New York's infrastructure and workforce

grants.title: New York Ventures Capital Programs
grants.summary: Funds aimed at supporting diverse entrepreneurs, fund managers, and accelerators to fuel New York’s innovation ecosystem.
grants.description: New York Ventures provides access to venture capital for high-growth companies, focusing on women, minority entrepreneurs, and undercapitalized regions. The program deploys over $300 million through equity programs for direct and indirect investments. It includes several funds such as Pre-seed and Seed Matching Fund, NYS Innovation Venture Capital Fund, Emerging and Regional Partner Fund, and Community and Regional Partner Fund. Interested applicants can submit their investment presentations for review.
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to diverse entrepreneurs, fund managers, and accelerators, including women and minority entrepreneurs.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Must be based in New York State
Focus on high-growth startups
Preference for women, minority, and veteran-led companies

grants.title: Office of Strategic Workforce Development Grant Programs
grants.summary: Funds industry-driven workforce training programs that provide in-demand skills and direct job placement in high-growth sectors.
grants.description: The Office of Strategic Workforce Development (OSWD) within Empire State Development (ESD) administers grant programs to support employer-driven, high-skilled workforce training programs. These programs aim to connect New Yorkers to quality jobs in priority industries, focusing on underserved populations such as un/underemployed and low-income individuals. Funding is available for capital expenses, programmatic costs, and wraparound services to reduce barriers to training. Applications are currently closed, with future funding rounds expected in 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include organizations offering workforce training programs aligned with priority industry sectors and providing industry-recognized credentials.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Programs must align with priority industry sectors.
Training must lead to industry-recognized credentials or certificates.
Focus on underserved populations, including un/underemployed individuals.
Provide wraparound services to reduce barriers to training.
Projects must meet minimum funding thresholds.

grants.title: Championing Albany's Potential (CAP) Initiative
grants.summary: A $400+ million initiative to revitalize the downtown core of Albany.
grants.description: The CAP Initiative, launched by Governor Hochul, aims to revitalize Albany's downtown core through a $400+ million investment. This initiative supports economic development, infrastructure improvements, and job creation in the Capital Region. Businesses and organizations in Albany can benefit from this funding to enhance their operations and contribute to the area's growth. Applications for funding are managed through the Consolidated Funding Application (CFA), which allows applicants to access multiple sources of funding through a single application process.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations and businesses in Albany's downtown core are eligible to apply.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Located in Albany's downtown core
Projects must align with economic development goals
Applications submitted through the CFA

grants.title: Opportunity Zone Program
grants.summary: This program encourages private investment in low-income urban and rural communities through designated Opportunity Zones.
grants.description: The Opportunity Zone Program in New York State is part of a federal initiative under the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017. It aims to stimulate private investment in underserved areas by designating low-income census tracts as Opportunity Zones. Investors can pool resources into Opportunity Funds to rebuild these communities, benefiting areas with high poverty rates and low median family incomes. New York State has recommended 514 census tracts for designation. Interested parties can find eligibility requirements and guidelines for Opportunity Funds through the U.S. Treasury. Applications and investments are managed at the federal and state levels.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations that can establish qualified Opportunity Funds and invest in designated Opportunity Zones are eligible.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Investors must establish qualified Opportunity Funds.
Funds must hold at least 90% of assets in qualified Opportunity Zone property.
Investments must be made in designated low-income census tracts.
